page: Microsoft Azure E20as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eav6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing also demonstrate strong performance. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests, such as uncached read and write operations, show average throughput, as do database operations and Redis SET operations. Large-scale LLM inference using a 70B model is a notable bottleneck, registering weak performance at 5.46 tokens per second, whereas text generation on small 135M models is strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E20as_v6 offers high memory density per vCPU, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local storage. Given its 160.0 GB RAM capacity and strong single-core capabilities, this server is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems, data processing pipelines, and enterprise applications requiring high memory capacity without the overhead of dedicated local NVMe drives.
description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs across 10 physical cores, 160.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers strong single-core performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall in the average tier. The server exhibits strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing, but struggles with large-scale LLM inference. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, the E20as_v6 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for in-memory caching, relational databases, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 20 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, making it ideal for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor
160.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
No local storage, but compatible with Premium Storage options
Suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data processing
